name: ian-pdf-pro
description: A cross-platform PDF suite — viewer, organizer, annotation, forms, and OCR — for Windows and Android. Built on Tauri v2, Rust, and React 19.

name: confirmation-outlook
description: A deployed supply-chain risk service, built end to end — a Python engine, a FastAPI backend, an MCP server, and a React operator console in one container. Risk is a conditional probability table of measured week-to-week transition frequencies, validated against a week the engine never saw: no fitted model, and it never claims one. The image generates its own synthetic world at build time and fails 42 assertions rather than ship wrong.

name: bom-workbench
description: A zero-dependency Python toolkit for BOM/MRP data work — multi-level BOM explosion, where-used lookup, and production-plan churn analysis over plain CSV, with a typed core, a full pytest suite, and fictional demo data.

name: command-center-os-template
description: A local-first ops console you run yourself — Next.js, TypeScript, and React 19 over a plain-JSON file database with live SSE and an agent-assignment queue. The de-identified template of the private Command Center OS I run my whole operation from.

name: Hermes-Claude-Brain-Vault
description: A drillable C4 architecture diagram of my Hermes second-brain, generated straight from its own infrastructure-as-code.

name: appliediqsolutions-site
description: The public front-end of AppliedIQ Solutions — Astro, React islands, and Tailwind on Cloudflare — with interactive supply-chain tool demos running on fictional data.

name: ian-provencher-site
description: The public front-end of this very site — a section-registry-driven Astro build that renders reviewed, public-safe snapshots of the real infrastructure I run.
